raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased event tickets totaling {$1300.00} using my American Express Delta XXXX XXXX for a XXXX XXXX XXXX yacht event scheduled for XX/XX/year>. 

The event promoter hosted two separate yacht events occurring simultaneously on the same date and time. Upon realizing the ticket purchased was for the incorrect yacht, I contacted the merchant on XX/XX/year>. During that call, I was advised to purchase the correct tickets and was told the original tickets would be refunded. Relying on that representation, I immediately purchased replacement tickets totaling {$1400.00}. 

The merchant later refused to issue a refund for the original {$1300.00} transaction and classified it as a no-show, despite it being impossible to attend two simultaneous events. 

I filed a dispute with American Express under services not rendered. The dispute was resolved in the merchants favor. I requested reconsideration ( Dispute References XXXX and XXXX ), but American Express maintained the denial , stating that written confirmation of a refund was required. 

My complaint is not based solely on a refund promise. The core issue is merchant misrepresentation and impossibility of performance. The original service could not be rendered because the merchant directed me to purchase alternate tickets for a concurrent event. Even absent a written refund confirmation, it is factually impossible for a consumer to attend two simultaneous events. 

I believe American Express improperly categorized and evaluated this dispute under a refund/no-show framework rather than under services not rendered or merchant misrepresentation. I am requesting regulatory review of American Expresss dispute handling and a reversal of the {$1300.00} charge.
